channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Wednesday it will permanently block its border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures to cope with “confrontational hysteria" by South Korean and U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes another arms depot inside Russia, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian drone struck an important arms depot inside Russia, the Ukraine military said Wednesday, three weeks after another drone blasted a major Russian armory and three days after a drone smashed into a key oil terminal in Russia-occupied Crimea.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rights groups ask Filipinos to reject ex-Pres Duterte and detained preacher in upcoming elections
item.description: Human rights activists called on Filipino voters to reject former President Rodrigo Duterte and detained televangelist Apollo Quiboloy in next year’s mid-term elections, citing the criminal allegations they’re facing.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nobel Prize in chemistry awarded to 3 scientists for work on proteins, building blocks of life
item.description: The Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences has awarded the Nobel Prize in chemistry to David Baker, Demis Hassabis and John Jumper for their work with proteins.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Niemann And Mike Corder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Survivors of massacre in small Haitian town where 70 died point finger at government
item.description: PONT-SONDÉ, Haiti (AP) — Angry whispers have broken the heavy silence that fell over Pont-Sondé just days after a vicious gang attack left more than 70 dead, marking one of Haiti’s biggest massacres in recent history.
item.dc:creator: PIERRE-RICHARD LUXAMA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japan's new PM dissolves the lower house of parliament to set up a snap election
item.description: New Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba has dissolved the lower house of parliament to set up an Oct. 27 snap election.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hezbollah steps up rocket fire as Israel sends more troops into Lebanon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah fired another barrage of rockets into Israel on Tuesday, and the militant group’s acting leader vowed to keep up pressure that has forced tens of thousands of Israelis from their homes near the Lebanese border.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk's X to be reinstated in Brazil after complying with Supreme Court demands
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — The Brazilian Supreme Court's Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Tuesday authorized the restoration of social media platform X´s service in Brazil, over a month after its nationwide shutdown, according to a court document that was made public. Elon Musk’s X was blocked on Aug.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dominican Republic starts mass deportations of Haitians and expels nearly 11,000 in a week
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The Dominican Republic said Tuesday it has deported or repatriated nearly 11,000 Haitians in the past week, fulfilling a pledge to do so weekly as neighboring Haiti scrambles to handle the influx while besieged by gang violence and poverty.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Israel plots to strike Iran, its choices range from symbolic to severe
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel has promised to retaliate for Iran’s massive missile attack last week. How it does so involves great risk, and could have major repercussions for the archenemies, the Middle East and the world.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France’s minority government survives a no-confidence vote, 2 weeks after taking office
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s minority government survived a no-confidence vote on Tuesday, two weeks after taking office, getting over the first hurdle placed by left-wing lawmakers to bring down new conservative Prime Minister Michel Barnier.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MI5 spy chief says Russia and Iran are behind a 'staggering' rise in deadly plots
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain is facing a “staggering rise” in attempts at assassination, sabotage and other crimes on U.K. soil by Russia and Iran, as the two states recruit criminals to “do their dirty work,” the head of Britain's domestic intelligence agency said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary’s Orban interrupted, accused of 'selling out' country to Russia, China during EU news conference
item.description: Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban was interrupted at a news conference by an activist and municipal councilor from the opposition Democratic Coalition party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Taiwan leader Tsai to visit Czech Republic, other European destinations, drawing China's ire
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Former Taiwan leader Tsai Ing-wen is to visit Czech Republic, and other European destinations, drawing China’s ire.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German court acquits McCann suspect of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: BRAUNSCHWEIG, Germany (AP) — A German court on Tuesday acquitted a man who is also under investigation in the 2007 disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann in a trial on charges of unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: FANNY BRODERSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Water gushes through palm trees and sand dunes after rare rain in the Sahara Desert
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — A rare deluge of rainfall left blue lagoons of water amid the palm trees and sand dunes of the Sahara desert, nourishing some of its most drought-stricken regions with more water than many had seen in decades.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ and BABA AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Votes are being counted in the election for a truncated government in Indian-controlled Kashmir
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Votes were being counted Tuesday in the recent election for a largely powerless local government in Indian-controlled Kashmir, the first since Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s government stripped the disputed region of its special status five years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Czech government undergoes a reshuffle after a party leaves but retains a majority in Parliament
item.description: PRAGUE (AP) — Czech President Petr Pavel swore in two new ministers on Tuesday as part of a reshuffle of the government following the decision of junior coalition partner , the Pirates, to leave the Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli military warns of operations on southern Lebanon's coast
item.description: Israel's military on Monday said it would soon launch operations on Lebanon’s southern coast, which would mark a further escalation of its fight against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Mexican mayor murdered less than a week after taking office
item.description: Mayor Alejandro Arcos of Chilpancingo, Mexico, has been confirmed dead less than a week after being sworn into the notoriously dangerous city's highest office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch: Trump delivers remarks on Oct. 7
item.description: Former President Trump took part in an event in Miami on Monday night to mark the anniversary of Hamas's attack against Israel. Earlier in the day, Trump visited the grave site of an Orthodox Jewish rabbi in New York City.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Schumer: Oct. 7 'will go down in infamy'
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.), the highest-ranking Jewish elected leader in American history, marked the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks in Israel as a “day that will go down in infamy” and pledged America “will never forget.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: How many are dead, missing one year since Hamas' attack on Israel?
item.description: Oct. 7 marks the first anniversary of Hamas’ attack on Israel, which prompted an Israeli invasion of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah rocket attack on Israel hits Haifa for first time
item.description: Hezbollah missiles landed in Haifa for the first time on Monday, prompting a swift response from the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saving lives on 'Death Street,' how an Israeli kindergarten teacher became a battlefield hero on October 7
item.description: When Hamas terrorists attacked her hometown in Southern Israel, Tali Hadad a kindergarten teacher, leapt into action to save 13 people, including her own wounded son.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American father of Hamas hostage Itay Chen pushes US, Israel on ‘Plan B’ as negotiations falter
item.description: The father of an American held by Hamas is urging the Biden administration to think outside of the box and return his son and others from Gaza as Israel's year-long strategy fails to free them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu calls Macron, other Western leaders who support arms embargo against Israel a ‘disgrace’
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu slammed Western leaders on Saturday who said they support an arms embargo on Israel over how its handling the Gaza war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rising from the ashes: Israelis in nation's war-torn south move home a year after October 7 massacre
item.description: The unique communal structure of the kibbutz plays a vital role in fostering resilience and support as people heal from the devastating impact of the October 7 massacre with many now returning home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor and mentor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: TERESA DE MIGUEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
